cases.title: Build one complete independently bootable service module
cases.prompt: Design an accounts preferences service module from endpoint definition through handler, registries, domain/data seams, one composition root, OpenAPI, standalone boot, and executable requests. It may later trigger workflows but must not invent them now.
rubric: Does not create workflow folders without durable behavior
cases.rationale: A complete module is a reachable deployment slice, not a definition and handler pair.

cases.title: Find a documented endpoint missing from the composed service
cases.prompt: Review an API where OpenAPI contains create-import, its definition and handler exist, the group registry omits it, and startup only warns about unmatched handlers. Diagnose the capability honestly and give correction and verification evidence.
rubric: Does not equate OpenAPI with implementation
Requires registry conformance and an executable request
cases.rationale: Held-out review must detect an unreachable contract despite convincing source files.

cases.title: Define complete multi-source request and response contracts
cases.prompt: Define a PATCH endpoint that reads organization and resource params, repeated query fields, a lowercased conditional header, and JSON. Include validation middleware, typed normalized input, success and problem variants, and OpenAPI compatibility checks.
cases.rationale: Schema-first design still fails when source and response semantics are flattened.

cases.title: Detect a schema-compatible semantic API break
cases.prompt: A collection endpoint keeps the same OpenAPI schema but changes default ordering, cursor interpretation, count from exact filtered to table estimate, and operationId. Decide compatibility and the release tests required.
rubric: Does not rely on schema diff alone
cases.rationale: Held-out coverage protects semantic compatibility, not keyword presence.

cases.title: Compose an Effect service graph with owned lifetimes
cases.prompt: Create an Effect architecture for a Hono accounts service using Context.Tag capabilities, typed domain errors, database and provider Layers, Scope/finalizers, validated config, observability, one host runtime, and test Layers.
rubric: Maps typed errors at the transport boundary
cases.rationale: The skill should produce an executable dependency and lifetime architecture, not Effect vocabulary.

cases.title: Repair a runtime and pool rebuilt per request
cases.prompt: A Hono middleware builds ManagedRuntime.make(AppLive) for every request; AppLive acquires a database pool and telemetry exporter. Tests pass but production connections grow and shutdown hangs. Diagnose and redesign lifetime, cancellation, and tests.
rubric: Moves runtime to the composition root
Connects request cancellation without closing shared resources
cases.rationale: Held-out resource pressure reveals incorrect Layer ownership.

cases.title: Enforce Better Auth organization policy in server queries
cases.prompt: Design a Better Auth-backed route for /organizations/:organization_id/imports/:import_id using paired plugins, import-safe construction, exact mount/cookie settings, membership policy, and a server-owned scoped database query.
rubric: Keeps plugin and deployment bindings aligned
cases.rationale: Organization IDs supplied by clients never become authority by validation alone.

cases.title: Handle organization revocation during a live session
cases.prompt: A user switched organizations in the UI and their membership was revoked in another tab, but a valid session cookie remains. They request an export and open its SSE stream. Define policy, query scope, stream authorization, and tests.
rubric: Does not treat session validity as current membership
cases.rationale: Held-out revocation tests organization authority beyond login.

cases.title: Own middleware order and one failure boundary
cases.prompt: Plan root, service, and route middleware for correlation, proxy policy, access logging, CORS, auth, organization policy, validators, handler, and error completion. Account for onion response order and one diagnostic per failure.
rubric: Places middleware at explicit ownership levels
cases.rationale: Framework registration order alone does not prove runtime response order.

cases.title: Propagate request disconnect without cancelling accepted durable work
cases.prompt: A client disconnects during a database search, an upload decode, and immediately after receiving 202 for a durable import. Define cancellation for each operation and prove resource cleanup.
rubric: Tests cleanup and downstream abort propagation
cases.rationale: Cancellation authority changes at durable acceptance.

cases.title: Design a durable authorized SSE progress feed
cases.prompt: Design /organizations/:org/imports/:run/events with a safe event union, opaque cursor, Last-Event-ID replay, high-water handoff, bounded delivery, disconnect cleanup, and authorization.
cases.rationale: A useful stream skill must connect wire framing to durable and authorization contracts.

cases.title: Recover an SSE endpoint with unbounded buffering
cases.prompt: An SSE endpoint pushes every workflow history item into an in-memory array before writing, accepts arbitrary run IDs, and cancels the workflow when the socket closes. Diagnose and specify corrections and load tests.
cases.rationale: Held-out pressure and authority failures must change the design.

cases.title: Make one API service independently deployable
cases.prompt: Turn an import-safe API package into an independently deployable service. Define entrypoint, config, resources, migrations, readiness, worker dependency, shutdown, OpenAPI, rollout compatibility, and artifact verification.
rubric: Distinguishes a library from deployable host
Includes worker reachability for accepted work
cases.rationale: Service boundaries must be executable and operational, not folder conventions.

cases.title: Reject false readiness for an accepted-work API
cases.prompt: The API readiness endpoint returns 200 after Hono starts even when the workflow SQL adapter is unsupported and no worker polls the queue. POST /imports still returns 202. Audit and redesign readiness and degraded behavior.
rubric: Does not return false durable acceptance
Makes capability status and degradation explicit
cases.rationale: Held-out deployment evidence protects truthfulness at the 202 boundary.

cases.title: Select a workflow engine from authority and recovery needs
cases.prompt: Choose among a scheduled command, Postgres job table, custom Effect control plane, @effect/workflow, and Temporal for a 30-day import/review process. Map authority, identities, timers, signals, retries, operators, and versioning before recommending.
rubric: Does not choose by abstraction preference
cases.rationale: Engine selection must begin with authority and recovery, not library popularity.

cases.title: Reject a retrying promise as a durable workflow
cases.prompt: A service calls an Effect program with exponential retry in a detached fiber and labels it durable because errors are typed. Audit every durability claim and define the minimum safe alternative.
cases.rationale: Held-out language must not let Effect vocabulary manufacture durability.

cases.title: Gate an Effect workflow production adapter honestly
cases.prompt: A repo proves Workflow.make and WorkflowEngine.layerMemory in tests but its effect_sql adapter returns not-implemented for registration, identity, start, poll, interrupt, and resume. Plan the production adapter, Layers, worker, config, observability, and acceptance tests without claiming completion.
rubric: Separates proven memory behavior from missing durability
Requires full adapter operations and cross-process tests
cases.rationale: The exact uploaded stub is the strongest test of capability honesty.

cases.title: Repair an Effect durable-wait wake-up gap
cases.prompt: A wait router marks a wait timed out, then separately enqueues an Effect resume token, appends timeline, and marks the run pending. A crash after the first write leaves no active wait and no resume. Redesign and verify signal/timeout/cancel races.
cases.rationale: Held-out atomicity catches a source-grounded durability defect.

cases.title: Design a complete Temporal import workflow
cases.prompt: Design a Temporal TypeScript CSV import requiring upload parse, idempotent database batches, manual mapping approval, progress query, correction update, cancellation, schedule, retry/timeout/heartbeat, worker deployment, Continue-As-New, and Postgres projection.
cases.rationale: Temporal guidance must cover the whole runtime and operations system.

cases.title: Prevent a nondeterministic Temporal rollout
cases.prompt: A Temporal workflow deployment reorders Activities, imports a Node provider client into workflow code, changes an input field from optional to required, and removes old workers immediately. Build a safe upgrade and rollback plan.
rubric: Finds workflow sandbox and command-history defects
Maintains compatible workers and payload decoding
cases.rationale: Held-out rollout tests deterministic and compatibility reasoning.

cases.title: Design atomic workflow admission policies
cases.prompt: Define idempotency, concurrency, throttle, rate limit, debounce, batch, singleton, and priority for tenant-scoped workflow starts. Specify storage primitives, persisted resolved keys, statuses, and accepted response links.
cases.rationale: The uploaded definitions expose rich policy shapes that require executable semantics.

cases.title: Detect a new control plane bypassed by the public API
cases.prompt: Definitions, PostgreSQL tables, worker loops, and a new control plane exist, but POST /imports still calls an old in-process dispatcher. Audit capability status and prove the corrected reachability path.
rubric: Reports the new system as unreachable
Requires an actual deployed request-to-effect trace
cases.rationale: Held-out topology prevents source presence from becoming a false completion claim.

cases.title: Close a database-to-workflow dual-write gap
cases.prompt: Creating an import commits PostgreSQL, then starts a workflow. Design the transactional outbox, publisher lease, stable event identity, receiver inbox, ambiguous acknowledgement, and reconciliation path.
rubric: Commits domain row and intent together
cases.rationale: Outbox behavior must include claim, receiver, and repair, not only a table.

cases.title: Recover an ambiguous external effect
cases.prompt: A payment Activity times out after the provider may have charged the card, then retries. The workflow start had an idempotency key but the provider request did not. Define correction, reconciliation, and crash tests.
rubric: Does not confuse start and effect idempotency
Looks up ambiguous provider state before retry
cases.rationale: Held-out external effects are where at-least-once claims cause real harm.

cases.title: Implement multi-worker lease safety
cases.prompt: Design a PostgreSQL ready queue claimed by multiple workers. Include SKIP LOCKED or equivalent atomic claim, leases, heartbeats, fencing, retries, poison work, fairness, backpressure, readiness, and graceful drain.
cases.rationale: A worker reference should lead to concrete claim and lifecycle design.

cases.title: Reject interval math for calendar schedules
cases.prompt: A worker advances both intervals and cron schedules by adding interval_seconds to now. Product schedules use America/Toronto and must define DST, overlap, missed runs, catch-up, and backfill. Diagnose and test the correction.
rubric: Defines stable occurrence identity and publication atomicity
cases.rationale: The uploaded worker explicitly leaves cron expansion incomplete.

cases.title: Define a committed resumable pipeline checkpoint
cases.prompt: Design checkpoints for streaming WARC records into PostgreSQL and ClickHouse. Include input identity/hash, stage/schema versions, committed cursor, output manifests/hashes, required sink commits, resume, and incompatible input behavior.
rubric: Checkpoints committed output rather than attempted input
cases.rationale: Resume correctness depends on output and provenance, not a line counter.

cases.title: Prevent a late expired worker from committing
cases.prompt: Worker A pauses after its lease expires; Worker B recovers and completes the item; Worker A resumes and writes an older result. Design fencing, idempotency, compare-and-set, operator evidence, and a deterministic test.
rubric: Does not rely on lease expiry alone
Rejects stale commits and preserves audit evidence
cases.rationale: Held-out late completion tests the hardest lease invariant.

cases.title: Project workflow progress into a safe replayable event stream
cases.prompt: Design a safe customer progress stream from an internal workflow history. Separate engine history, operator timeline, domain events, public events, atomic publication, cursors, replay/live delivery, retention, and cancellation.
rubric: Does not expose raw engine history
Keeps HTTP delivery decoupled from workflow execution
cases.rationale: Workflow streaming needs an explicit projection contract before SSE.

cases.title: Handle an expired workflow event cursor honestly
cases.prompt: Public progress events retain seven days, a customer reconnects with a ten-day-old cursor, operator timeline retains 90 days, and raw Temporal history contains private payloads. Define safe resync behavior without silently starting at now or exposing raw history.
rubric: Uses a safe snapshot/reset or error contract
cases.rationale: Held-out retention requires truthful resynchronization semantics.
